key_insight: LAMG+ is a lean, parameter-free algebraic multigrid solver for graph Laplacians that is both highly robust and empirically linear-time, with targeted refinements that fix anisotropy-related failures.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical high-performance alternative for large sparse Laplacian systems in structural, finite-element, clustering, and network-flow workloads, especially where memory and robustness matter.
method: Julia re-derivation of Lean Algebraic Multigrid (LAMG)
Added selective caliber-2 interpolation to address grid-aligned anisotropy
evidence_signal: Across 1,711 SuiteSparse graphs, LAMG+ converged on 100% of cases with a median of 4 cycles and an empirical log-log scaling slope of 1.01, up to 2.4×10^8 nonzeros.
watch_item: Performance is problem-class dependent: LAMG+ is especially strong on finite-element/structural matrices, while approximate-Cholesky can be faster on social and citation graphs.
paper_title: LAMG+: A Robust Lean Algebraic Multigrid Solver for Graph Laplacians
